title: Engineering harness CLI envelope
description: The JSON object every harness CLI subcommand prints on stdout. Process exit codes 0/1/2 are layered on top: 0 when status=pass, 2 when status=unconfigured, 1 otherwise. The error.code enum lets callers branch without parsing the human-readable message.

command.description: Subcommand name as the user invoked it (e.g. "doctor", "validate", "onboard").

status.description: High-level outcome. pass=worked; fail=ran and failed; unconfigured=cannot run because config missing (exit 2); degraded=ran with reduced guarantees (e.g. neither-runtime install path); dry-run=did not actually execute; running=async still in flight (rare); skipped=intentionally not run; unknown=fallback.

data.description: Optional structured payload specific to the subcommand. Schema is per-subcommand and documented in templates/cli-command-contract.md. Free-form JSON; never null.

error.description: Present when status in {fail, unconfigured, degraded}. Absent on pass/dry-run/skipped.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. See templates/cli-command-contract.md for per-subcommand subsets.

message.description: Human-readable, one-sentence error description. No stack traces; those go in messages[].

next_action.description: Optional one-line suggestion for the user or agent on how to recover (e.g. "Run engineering-harness-setup to materialise the onboarding guide.").

messages.description: Optional log lines, warnings, and stack traces. Free-form strings, ordered by emission time.
